keto_effect: Not keto-specific; more relevant for folate adequacy when diet variety is restricted.
description: This locus is commonly discussed as C677T. Folate handling may differ by genotype, so this is better used for methylation context than as a keto predictor.

keto_effect: May relate to fat-loss response during carbohydrate restriction, but evidence is not strong enough for deterministic use.
description: Useful as a low-confidence exploratory marker only.

nutrition_relevance: Adipocyte biology, insulin sensitivity, fat metabolism.
keto_effect: May relate to metabolic flexibility, but not a high-confidence keto predictor.
description: Best treated as contextual rather than prescriptive.

keto_effect: More useful for appetite/weight context than ketone production itself.
description: A well-studied obesity locus that may affect eating behavior and weight-loss dynamics.

keto_effect: Exploratory only.
description: Insufficient evidence for practical use in a keto panel.

keto_effect: Exploratory only.
description: Not well established for consumer nutrition guidance.

keto_effect: Exploratory only.
description: Evidence is sparse and not keto-specific.

keto_effect: Possible lipid-response context, not a validated keto marker.
description: Can be retained as an exploratory SNP.

keto_effect: Especially relevant if keto is high in butter, cream, cheese, or other saturated-fat-heavy foods.
description: This is one of the more practical fat-response SNPs for nutrition use.

keto_effect: May be more relevant to fat quality and insulin sensitivity than ketosis itself.
description: Often included in nutrition panels, but should not be overinterpreted.

keto_effect: Potentially relevant for tolerance and calorie density on a high-fat diet.
description: The Thr54-related signal is often interpreted as increased fat absorption efficiency.

keto_effect: Interesting but not strong enough for firm recommendation logic.
description: Keep as exploratory.

keto_effect: Potentially relevant to carbohydrate restriction adaptation, but still exploratory.
description: Useful as low-confidence context only.

nutrition_relevance: Glucose regulation and type 2 diabetes risk context.
keto_effect: Useful for carb-sensitivity and glycemic-monitoring context rather than as a direct ketosis marker.
description: One of the strongest common type 2 diabetes loci in nutrigenetics.
source: Gene-diet interaction meta-analyses and nutrigenetic reviews.

keto_effect: More about fat quality and PUFA balance than ketone production.
description: Associated with altered desaturase activity and circulating PUFA patterns.

keto_effect: Relevant if the diet leans on carotenoid-rich foods instead of preformed vitamin A sources.
description: Lower conversion efficiency can matter in broader nutrition planning.

keto_effect: Not keto-specific; useful for micronutrient interpretation.
description: Often considered together with other BCMO1 variants.

keto_effect: Useful if evaluating a high-fat pattern alongside liver health and choline sufficiency.
description: May help flag people who need closer attention to choline intake.

keto_effect: Not keto-specific; broadly useful in nutrition monitoring.
description: Frequently associated with lower circulating vitamin D status.

keto_effect: Very practical if keto food choices include substantial dairy.
description: Classic lactase-persistence marker widely used in nutrition genetics.

keto_effect: Useful if coffee, caffeine, or MCT-coffee routines are part of the diet pattern.
description: A major common determinant of caffeine clearance rate.

nutrition_relevance: Lipid handling; interpret together with rs7412.
keto_effect: Useful for caution around LDL/non-HDL response on high-fat diets, especially if saturated fat is high.
description: Should be paired with rs7412 to infer APOE isoform context.

nutrition_relevance: Lipid handling; interpret together with rs429358.
keto_effect: Best used as part of APOE isoform context rather than standalone.
description: Combines with rs429358 to define APOE isoforms.

keto_effect: Sometimes included in weight-loss panels, but evidence is mixed.
description: Do not use as a strong decision SNP.

keto_effect: Low-confidence contextual marker only.
description: Evidence is inconsistent across cohorts.

keto_effect: Potentially relevant to weight-loss resistance, not a keto-specific predictor.
description: Best treated as exploratory.

keto_effect: Useful for adherence/appetite context rather than ketone production.
description: A practical appetite-related SNP for behavior-aware nutrition interpretation.
